childDosage.name: Child Dosage Calculator
childDosage.description: Weight-based paediatric dosing for paracetamol, ibuprofen and amoxicillin.

meta.title: Child Dosage Calculator — Weight-Based Paediatric Drug Dosing | Health Calculators
meta.description: Calculate safe single-dose and max daily dose of paracetamol, ibuprofen and amoxicillin by child weight. Includes volume in mL. Not medical advice.

childDosage.title: Child Dosage Calculator
childDosage.description: Calculate the weight-based single dose and daily maximum of common paediatric medicines — paracetamol, ibuprofen, amoxicillin.
childDosage.concentrationHint: Optional — e.g. paracetamol syrup 24 mg/mL, ibuprofen syrup 20 mg/mL.
childDosage.formulaText: Calculation: {weight} kg × {mgPerKg} mg/kg = {dose} mg per single dose.

warnings.weightTooLow: Weight below 3 kg — neonates require clinician-supervised dosing.
warnings.weightTooHigh: Weight above 80 kg — please use adult dosing.
warnings.belowMinWeight: Below recommended minimum weight for this drug. Confirm with a clinician before use.
warnings.overdose: Overdose warning: requested mg/kg exceeds recommended single dose.

childDosage.disclaimer: Important: this is not medical advice. Paediatric dosing must be confirmed by a licensed clinician or pharmacist.

faq.a: The standard formula is dose (mg) = body weight (kg) × mg per kg. Paracetamol uses 10–15 mg/kg per single dose, ibuprofen 7–10 mg/kg. These come from paediatric prescribing references.

faq.q: How many mL of syrup should I give?
faq.a: Volume (mL) = dose (mg) ÷ concentration (mg/mL). Example: 300 mg paracetamol at 24 mg/mL = 12.5 mL of syrup.

faq.q: What is the maximum daily dose?
faq.a: Paracetamol: max 60 mg/kg/day (or 4 g for older children). Ibuprofen: max 30 mg/kg/day (or 2.4 g). Amoxicillin: 20–90 mg/kg/day depending on indication.

faq.q: From what age are these drugs appropriate?
faq.a: Paracetamol: from birth (with adjusted dosing). Ibuprofen: from 3 months and ≥5 kg. Amoxicillin: from birth with paediatric adjustment. For infants under 3 months always consult a clinician.

faq.q: What should I do in case of accidental overdose?
faq.a: Call poison control or emergency services immediately. Paracetamol overdose can cause severe liver damage even when initial symptoms are absent.

faq.q: Does this calculator replace medical advice?
faq.a: No. The calculator is a plausibility tool. The final dose must always be confirmed by a licensed clinician or pharmacist.
